title: Velvety Earthy Algerian Chicken Stir-fry
description: A lazy Sunday dish featuring tender chicken pieces in a rich, earthy sauce that's both vegetarian-friendly and perfect for those who enjoy a hearty but not-too-complicated meal.

ingredients.name: garlic cloves
ingredients.notes: use more for a stronger flavor

ingredients.name: tomato paste
ingredients.notes: may be omitted for lighter version

ingredients.name: thyme leaves
ingredients.notes: dried or fresh, optional but enhances the earthiness

instructions: Season the chicken pieces with salt and pepper on both sides.
Drizzle olive oil over the diced aubergine, place in an oven-safe dish, and bake for about 15 minutes or until tender but not mushy.
Heat a bit of olive oil in a pan. Add the onion and garlic, sautéing until soft and translucent.
Add bell peppers to the pan, tossing well with the onions and garlic. Season with salt and fresh lemon juice for freshness.
Pour in tomato paste (or increase if you like), mix thoroughly, then add deglazed pan juices from the chicken after browning on the sides.
Once everything is combined and heated through, place the baked aubergine around the edges of the pan to create a bed, then add the cooked chicken pieces.
Mix in the thyme leaves (if using), paprika (for color if needed), and allow it all to simmer gently for 10-15 minutes until flavors meld together.
Serve hot with crusty bread or as a side dish with couscous.
